summary: Lead accepts M6-SUCCESSION-COMPLETION-001 after systems_architect R2 ACCEPT and lead integration gates. The implementation completes deterministic Alpha succession resolution, abdication-like triggers, peaceful/regency/disputed outcomes, M6 legitimacy integration, malformed save/invariant rejection, one-candidate low-support structured rejection, and event-tail save/load preservation within allowed paths.

tests_run.result: 24 files passed; 186 tests passed; existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning only.
Node/worker-compatible hashes matched for hello, command/query, save/load, M4, and M5 canaries.
Architecture boundaries passed; sim determinism passed; root Vitest 37 files / 287 tests passed; web 1 file / 4 tests passed.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture:check passed.

decisions: ACCEPT systems_architect R2 review for M6 succession completion.
Regency is accepted as an M3/M6 Alpha temporary stewardship outcome using existing M3 succession state; no separate office/social system is introduced.
Low-support single-candidate disputed succession is a structured command rejection, preserving existing support-threshold semantics and avoiding invalid authoritative state.
Succession legitimacy effects reuse existing M6 legitimacy source/profile state and reason codes; no duplicate diplomacy/legitimacy system is introduced.
No Human Gate is active; manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and untouched.
risks: Succession legitimacy magnitudes are deterministic Alpha constants and may be tuned by later balance tasks.
Downstream M6 start-to-victory validation must still prove recognized-order victory flow using these surfaces.
requested_action: Commit accepted implementation, open PR, wait for CI, merge to main, then close task.
